headline: Agent memory and long-running execution patterns moved to the front of the quarter, while workflow automation cooled despite the highest ratio reading among movers.
body: The dominant rotation this quarter ran through agent persistence: Hermes added a unified memory store and skill library that survives Claude session resets, and a related framework paired persistent memory with cron scheduling and self-written skills. Google Cloud Tech codified three architectural patterns for long-running agents (event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation), all surfacing as the supporting structure under subagents. Workflow automation carried the largest ratio at 101 but is now weakening, and agent harness, scoped skills, AI-assisted development, and PIV Loop all read as lagging. New entrants including Distillation, latent caching, delegation chains, and bottleneck architecture broke out without yet anchoring to items in the window.

cited_items.title: Hermes Agent Tutorial: How to Setup & Use Open Source AI Agent for Beginners
3 patterns to build long-running AI agents
